steps.title: Use the fractional exponent rule
steps.content: A fractional exponent with numerator $1$ means a root: $$x^{1/n}=\sqrt[n]{x}.$$ Here $n=14$, so $$x^{1/14}=\sqrt[14]{x}.$$

steps.title: Check against the choices
steps.content: Choice B is correct. Choice A treats the exponent like multiplication by $\dfrac{1}{14}$, choice C multiplies by $14$, and choice D uses the reciprocal exponent idea incorrectly by raising $x$ to the $14$th power.
